In this episode:

In Episode 220 of The Bitcoin Fortress Podcast, we examine the most important Bitcoin developments from June 7ΓÇô14, 2026. We discuss the return of ETF inflows, the growing dominance of BlackRock and Fidelity in the Bitcoin ETF market, StrategyΓÇÖs latest Bitcoin purchase and the evolving treasury-company landscape, mining profitability and the recent difficulty adjustment, stablecoin regulation under the GENIUS Act, the ongoing CLARITY Act debate, and the broader implications of AI, surveillance, and digital freedom. We also explore the major themes emerging from the Bitcoin podcast universe, including inflation, private-company Bitcoin treasuries, operational security, and post-quantum Bitcoin research. Finally, we feature A Bitcoin Way of Life ΓÇô Week 4, reflect on Bitcoin as a discipline rather than merely an investment, and close with a reminder that while the fiat world grows increasingly complex, Bitcoin continues to do exactly what it was designed to doΓÇöproduce blocks and provide a foundation for financial sovereignty.

item.title: THE Bitcoin Podcast ΓÇô The Praxeology of Privacy: Freedom Tech vs Mass Surveillance | Max Hillebrand
item.description:
Privacy isnΓÇÖt about having something to hide. ItΓÇÖs about preserving the freedom to act, communicate, and coordinate without coercion.

In this episode of THE Bitcoin Podcast, Walker sits down with privacy advocate, cypherpunk, and Freedom Tech builder Max Hillebrand to discuss his new book, The Praxeology of Privacy.

Max explains how Austrian economics and cypherpunk philosophy intersect, why privacy is a natural consequence of self-ownership and property rights, and how surveillance distorts economic calculation in much the same way that money printing distorts markets.

The conversation explores:

  • Why privacy is fundamentally tied to freedom
  • The economic consequences of surveillance
  • CBDCs and the future of financial control
  • Why corporations and governments increasingly operate as surveillance partners
  • The role of Bitcoin in creating a parallel economy
  • Tor, VPNs, encryption, and network-level privacy
  • NostrΓÇÖs growing ecosystem and why developer adoption matters more than user counts
  • Operational security (OpSec) in both the digital and physical world
  • SignalΓÇÖs limitations and the future of decentralized private communications
  • White Noise, Marmot, and the next generation of unstoppable encrypted messaging
  • How Freedom Tech can make coercion more expensive and liberty more accessible

Max also shares his vision for a future where money, communication, identity, and publishing are all decentralized, permissionless, and resistant to censorship.

Whether youΓÇÖre new to privacy or already deep down the cypherpunk rabbit hole, this conversation provides a powerful framework for understanding why privacy mattersΓÇöand what practical steps you can take to reclaim it.

FOLLOW MAX:

NOSTR: https://primal.net/maxhillebrand

Try White Noise Chat: https://www.whitenoise.chat/

https://towardsliberty.com/

GET HIS BOOK:

The Praxeology of Privacy on Amazon: https://tinyurl.com/2xn9bs8y

Read on Nostr: https://tinyurl.com/4b42kb7d

  • BLOCKSTREAM JADE: BLOCKSTREAM JADE HARDWARE WALLET: Head to https://store.blockstream.com/ and use coupon code WALKER for 10% off!

item.title: Bitcoin Magazine Podcast ΓÇô Calle on Bitchat: Messaging When The Internet is Shut Down
item.description:
What happens to communication when the internet gets shut off? Frank Corva sits down with Calle ΓÇö creator of Cashu and a contributor to BitChat ΓÇö to unpack two of the most exciting open-source projects in Bitcoin. They dig into how Cashu ecash matured from experimental tech into a real economy, and why BitChat's Bluetooth mesh network took off in Jamaica, Iran, and Nepal. Calle also explains why ecash may become the native money of AI agents.



Grab your copy THE 2036 ISSUE≡ƒæçhttps://store.bitcoinmagazine.com/pages/the-2036-issue

≡ƒö╢ Frank Corva - Independent Content Producer≡ƒö╢ Creator of Cashu and contributor to BitChat



Chapters:


00:56 ΓÇö The state of Cashu ecash and building the foundation

02:07 ΓÇö Who's actually adopting ecash and why

03:26 ΓÇö Ecash as "pocket money" for AI agents

04:50 ΓÇö BitChat goes global: Jamaica, Iran, Nepal

05:35 ΓÇö Why "build it and they will come" isn't a law

07:45 ΓÇö Staying connected when the internet is shut down

09:00 ΓÇö How far BitChat's mesh network can really reach

11:32 ΓÇö Nostr, geohash chats, and going online

12:46 ΓÇö What's next: open-source AI agents



#Bitcoin #Cashu #BitChat #Ecash #Calle #MITBitcoinExpo #BitcoinPrivacy #MeshNetworking #Nostr #OpenSource #Bluetooth #AIagents #BitcoinMagazine #Satoshis #Privacy #DecentralizedTech #FreeSpeech #LightningNetwork #Meshtastic #BitcoinNews #SelfSovereign #BitcoinLayer2 #ZeusWallet #OfflineMessaging


DISCLAIMER: The views and opinions expressed in this show are those of the participants and do not necessarily reflect the official policy or position of BTC Inc., Bitcoin Magazine, or any affiliated entities. This content is provided for informational and educational purposes only and should not be construed as investment, legal, tax, or accounting advice. Nothing contained in this show constitutes a solicitation, recommendation, endorsement, or offer to buy or sell any securities or financial instruments. Viewers should consult their own advisors before making financial or business decisions.

item.title: The Bitcoin Treasury Podcast ΓÇô The Hidden Cost of a Large Bitcoin Buy
item.description:
The most common mistake in a first Bitcoin allocation is not the price paid. It is assuming the execution will take care of itself.

Teams make the buy decision, line up the approval, and then route a seven-figure order through the same retail venue they would use to buy a few hundred dollars of Bitcoin. The order fills, but not at the price they saw. The gap between the quoted price and what they actually paid can be significant, and on a large order it is real money.

Buying Bitcoin is operationally straightforward. Buying it well, at size, is not.

Michael Geraci, derivatives trader at Secure Digital Markets, covers the exchange, execution, and OTC relationships institutional Bitcoin holders need to understand before the first dollar moves. Will Reeves, CEO of Fold, joins with the operatorΓÇÖs view from running a public company that holds Bitcoin on its own balance sheet.

ΓÇ£At a million dollars and up is really where the OTC markets offer depth and liquidity.ΓÇ¥┬á ΓÇö Michael Geraci, Secure Digital Markets

What this session covers

When a retail exchange stops being enough

  • ┬á ┬á Past a certain size, execution cost becomes real. Slippage becomes material around $250K in notional and is unambiguous past $1M, where retail order books cannot absorb the trade cleanly.
  • ┬á ┬á A $1M Bitcoin buy typically runs 5 to 60 bps in execution cost. On a single trade that looks small; across a recurring accumulation program it is the difference that decides your cost basis.
  • ┬á ┬á OTC desks exist to solve this, offering real depth, bilateral pricing, and a counterparty accountable on the other end of the phone.

How a large order actually gets worked

  • ┬á ┬á Size is worked, not clicked. A meaningful order is sliced across venues and over time rather than dropped into a single book that it would move against itself.
  • ┬á ┬á Execution is benchmarked. Working an order against TWAP or VWAP lets it track the market instead of running ahead of it, which is the difference between a fill a treasurer can defend to a board and one they cannot.
  • ┬á ┬á Discretion matters. Bilateral pricing keeps a large order off the public tape, so the trade does not signal its own size to the rest of the market.

The operational groundwork that makes execution clean

  • ┬á ┬á Tier-1 banking relationships matter. Fiat needs to move cleanly, and wires to crypto venues are routinely flagged or blocked at banks that are not set up for them. Established banking rails remove that friction.
  • ┬á ┬á Work with a firm that picks up the phone. Setting up to trade at size involves real onboarding, banking, and compliance questions, and the answers are specific to each entity. A client-centric desk walks you through them with a named person on the other end of the line, rather than leaving you to a support inbox or a contact form.
  • ┬á ┬á Settlement speed is an edge. Pre-funded trades that settle T+0 to T+1 reduce the window in which anything can go wrong, rather than leaving funds in transit for days.
  • ┬á ┬á Flexibility is the point of a desk. Bespoke, bilateral structuring gives a treasury options a public order book cannot, from how an order is filled to how a position is later hedged or financed.

Get these pieces right and the first allocation stops being a leap of faith. The price you pay is defensible, the execution is clean, and you have a counterparty who answers when you call. From there, the same desk can help the position do more than sit, structuring yield, downside protection, and financing against Bitcoin you already hold, which is exactly the role a desk like SDM is built to play.

About Secure Digital Markets

SDM is a crypto-native institutional brokerage offering spot execution, derivatives, and structured lending. It specializes in bilateral transactions that give treasuries the depth, flexibility, and privacy a retail exchange cannot match, while clients retain custody of their own assets. You can learn more at SDM.co.

]]>

item.title: bitcoin ist - Wissen statt FOMO ΓÇô 18) ... Deflation f├╝r alle
item.description:
Warum schlagen ├ûkonomen und Zentralbanken sofort Alarm, wenn sie das Wort ΓÇ₧DeflationΓÇ£ h├╢ren? Sie warnen vor Wirtschaftskrisen, hoher Arbeitslosigkeit und dem Zusammenbruch der M├ñrkte. Und in unserem aktuellen Finanzsystem haben sie damit sogar recht ΓÇô allerdings tun sie damit dem Begriff der Deflation unrecht.

In dieser Folge von ΓÇ₧bitcoin ist...ΓÇ£ schauen wir uns den Begriff der Deflation genauer an. Denn es gibt zwei Arten von Deflation: Eine, die ein krankes System kollabieren l├ñsst, und eine, die der absolute Normalzustand der menschlichen Zivilisation sein sollte.

  • Der "Fehler" im System: Warum unser schuldenbasiertes Geldsystem Angst vor sinkenden Preisen hat und warum Kredite niemals ΓÇ₧teurerΓÇ£ werden d├╝rfen.

  • Die gute Deflation: Wie der technologische Fortschritt (vom Rad bis zum Internet) unser Leben nat├╝rlicherweise immer effizienter und g├╝nstiger machen m├╝sste.

  • Das 2%-Mantra: Warum Zentralbanken die Geldmenge k├╝nstlich ausweiten, um den Produktivit├ñtsgewinn deiner Lebenszeit unbemerkt abzusch├╢pfen.

  • Bitcoin als L├╢sung: Wie eine absolut begrenzte Geldmenge von 21 Millionen Einheiten daf├╝r sorgt, dass der menschliche Fortschritt endlich wieder direkt in deinem Geldbeutel landet.

ΓÇ₧Die Zentralbanken bek├ñmpfen die schlechte Deflation, indem sie permanent Geld drucken. Doch damit zerst├╢ren sie gleichzeitig die gute Deflation ΓÇô sie fressen den technologischen Fortschritt einfach auf und verw├ñssern unsere Kaufkraft schleichend.ΓÇ£

Alles von "bitcoin ist..." Hier geht es zu allen meinen Kan├ñlen: https://linktr.ee/bitcoinist

Einsteiger-Spezial: m├╢chtest du wissen, WIE man bitcoin sicher kauft und verwahrt? kostenlose und sichere Kaufanleitung direkt in dein Postfach

Hinterlasse mir gerne eine 5-Sterne-Bewertung oder einen Kommentar. Danke, Dein Christian.


]]>

item.title: The Bitcoin Way Podcast ΓÇô Top 5 Reasons To Get A Plan B Residency | Bitcoin Banter
item.description:
≡ƒôû Interested in a Plan B? Download our FREE Panama ≡ƒç╡≡ƒçª Residency Guide here:

≡ƒôû Subscribe to our newsletter: https://www.thebitcoinway.com/resources/newsletter?utmcampaign=bitcoin-banter&utmmedium=social&utm_source=fountain

ΓÇö------------------------

War, inflation, and growing government surveillance have made a ΓÇ£Plan BΓÇ¥ residency more essential than ever. In this special edition of Bitcoin Banter, Michael and Tony sit down with entrepreneur Adam Hudson to count down the top 5 reasons you should get a second residency, citizenship, or passport abroad. This episode breaks down how to build real freedom and financial sovereignty.

Drawing on AdamΓÇÖs own journey from Australia to Panama, the conversation covers offshore residency, second passports, territorial tax systems, self-custody Bitcoin, privacy and cybersecurity, and how everyday people (not just the rich) can create an exit plan in an increasingly restrictive world. Whether youΓÇÖre an Australian, a Bitcoiner, or anyone seeking more freedom and optionality, this is your roadmap to getting started.

ΓÇö------------------------

00:00 Intro

item.title: State of Bitcoin with Brandon Keys ΓÇô The European Bitcoin Ban Hiding In Plain Sight with Matyas Kuchar - State of Bitcoin Ep. 238
item.description:
Until last year, more than 1,000 restaurants in Czech Republic accepted Bitcoin. Then it all stopped.

The MiCA regulation didn't ban Bitcoin in Europe. It did something far more effective ΓÇö it made the rails impossible to operate. And as the Czech merchant economy quietly disappeared, two bigger forces started moving in the background: the petrodollar dying, and CBDCs coming online in Europe THIS YEAR.

In this episode, I sit down with Matyas Kuchar ΓÇö co-founder of BTC Prague, the largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe, and one of the most thoughtful voices on European Bitcoin policy ΓÇö to break down why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe, why the European surveillance state is spreading faster than Americans realize, and why he believes Bitcoin must win because the alternative is just so much worse.

You'll learn:

ΓåÆ How MiCA quietly killed 1,000+ Bitcoin-accepting restaurants in Czech Republic ΓÇö and why it wasn't even a direct ban

ΓåÆ Why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe (Trezor, Slush Pool, BIP-39, General Bytes ΓÇö all started in Prague)

ΓåÆ Why the petrodollar is dying ΓÇö and what the loss of US hegemony means for global decentralization

ΓåÆ Why Europeans use Bitcoin as money while Americans treat it as digital gold ΓÇö and which approach is right

ΓåÆ The European CBDC threat: pilots are launching THIS YEAR ΓÇö and why most Americans haven't heard about it

ΓåÆ The CBDC mechanism in Matyas's words: "You can analyze, you can control, you can surveil, you can send from a helicopter"

ΓåÆ Why even an anarcho-capitalist now meets with politicians ΓÇö and what changed his mind

ΓåÆ Why Bitcoin is no longer just savings ΓÇö it's the network of people building the future you actually want to live in

≡ƒÜ¿ JOIN ME AT BTC PRAGUE 2026

The largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe ΓÇö June 11-13. Use code GREENCANDLE for discounted tickets:

https://btcprague.com/

≡ƒÄ» PROTECT YOUR BITCOIN WITH SALT LENDING

Borrow against your Bitcoin without selling a single sat. Green Candle viewers get an exclusive offer:

https://saltlending.com/green-candle

≡ƒÄ» PLAN YOUR WEALTH WITH STRONG WEALTH

Build a Bitcoin-aligned wealth strategy ΓÇö estate planning, tax optimization, portfolio construction:

https://strongwealth.net/

≡ƒÄ» MINE BITCOIN WITH SIMPLE MINING

First-year repairs free for Green Candle listeners:

https://simplemining.io/green-candle

≡ƒôè REFERENCED IN THIS EPISODE

ΓÇó MiCA regulation (EU 2023/1114) ΓÇö Markets in Crypto-Assets framework

ΓÇó Czech Republic Bitcoin tax exemption (3-year hold rule)

ΓÇó Slush Pool (founded 2010 in Prague, first ever Bitcoin mining pool)

ΓÇó BIP-39 seed phrase standard (developed in Prague)

ΓÇó Trezor hardware wallet (first ever, founded in Prague)

ΓÇó EU CBDC pilot programs (launching 2026)

ΓÇó BTC Prague 2026 conference ΓÇö June 11-13

Bitcoin #BTCPrague #Matyas #MiCA #BitcoinEurope #CBDC #BitcoinPolicy #EuropeanBitcoin #BitcoinConference #SoundMoney #Petrodollar #BitcoinAdoption #CzechRepublic #FinancialFreedom #BitcoinNews

≡ƒöö SUBSCRIBE for weekly Bitcoin, macro, and personal finance content.

ΓÜá∩╕Å Disclaimer: This video is for informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Always do your own research.

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin News Alerts | Daily BTC Macro Signal ΓÇô 2372: $100T Bitcoin Race - Oil Nations Enter The Bitcoin Era
item.description:
In this episode, we explore why oil-producing nations entering Bitcoin may represent the next phase of adoption, how nation-state game theory is accelerating, and why Bitcoin is increasingly becoming strategic infrastructure rather than a speculative asset.

Strategy holds 846,842 BTC. BlackRock's IBIT holds 766,412 BTC. U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs control roughly 1.1 million BTC, and lawmakers are discussing a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve targeting 1,000,000 Bitcoin.

≡ƒô║ Prefer video? Watch daily Bitcoin breakdowns: http://BitcoinNewsAlerts.net

item.title: M├╝nzcrime - True Crime Bitcoin Podcast ΓÇô Unter Geldw├ñscheverdacht Episode 6 (mit Plep4Fun)
item.description:
Konto gesperrt. Komplett. Kein ├£berweisen, kein Abheben, kein gar nichts. Und das ohne Vorwarnung, ohne Erkl├ñrung, einfach so. 


Genau das hat Pleb4Fun im Januar 2025 erwischt. Von einer Sekunde auf die andere war er raus aus dem System ΓÇô w├ñhrend Rechnungen weiterhin kamen und das Leben sich nicht um sein Bankproblem scherte. 
Was dann folgte, war kein schneller Anruf mit happy ending. Es war ein b├╝rokratischer Alptraum, der eine viel unbequemere Frage aufwirft: Wie leicht ger├ñt man heutzutage eigentlich ins Visier der Beh├╢rden, obwohl man absolut nichts falsch gemacht hat? 


Chefermittler Mavi hat sich mit Pleb4Fun zusammengesetzt und die ganze Geschichte auseinandergenommen ΓÇô akribisch, ehrlich und ohne Sch├╢nf├ñrberei. Raus gekommen ist dabei eine Episode, die unter die Haut geht. Nicht weil sie rei├ƒerisch ist, sondern weil es Realit├ñt ist.


Geldw├ñscheverdacht. Ein Begriff, den die meisten Menschen mit Kriminellen verbinden. Nicht mit jemandem, der einfach seinen Alltag lebt. 
Das hier ist keine abstrakte Diskussion ├╝ber Finanzgesetze. Das ist die Geschichte eines Menschen, der pl├╢tzlich beweisen musste, dass er kein Verbrecher ist ΓÇô und das Protokoll dazu liegt jetzt auf dem Tisch. 

Links zur Folge:
Pleb4Fun Dokumentation auf X

Ticketverkauf Event Brivat (Bitcoin Privacy Event)

Der Fall Samourai

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Interviews ΓÇô Syed, Midnight President ΓÇö $200M Spent, Zero VCs, and Why Anonymity Isn't Privacy
item.description: <![CDATA[Fahmi Syed runs the Midnight Foundation, custodian of a fourth-generation privacy-first blockchain backed by Charles Hoskinson. Roughly $200 million spent. Zero VC funding. One of the biggest airdrops in crypto history.Jamie Redman sits down with Fahmi at Consensus 2026 for the full breakdown: programmable privacy, selective disclosure, the dual-token Night/Dust model, why Zcash and Monero keep getting delisted, why agentic AI desperately needs a proof layer, and what "Web 2.5" actually means.We cover:- The bank-account-number anecdote that defines rational privacy- Why transparent blockchains can't carry institutions across the bridge- Midnight's private-permissions model between Monero and JPMorgan- The dual-token Night/Dust design and predictable corporate costs- One of crypto's biggest airdrops ΓÇö no VCs, no insider allocations- March 2026 mainnet and the 100+ builders in pre-prod- The 2008 TradFi trading floor that shaped his self-custody viewsFilmed at Consensus 2026.Host: Jamie Redman]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Audible ΓÇô The TRUTH about Iran's "Bitcoin" seizure
item.description:
I break down the TreasuryΓÇÖs claim that it seized $1B of IranΓÇÖs "crypto" and explain why this was actually Tether, not Bitcoin ΓÇô and why that distinction matters.

I digs into how stablecoins, ETFs, and exchanges recreate the same centralized, confiscable banking system, contrasted with true Bitcoin self-custody.

When you consider Iran, Russia, and Afghanistan as case studies, it is clear that every other reserve asset becomes politicized. The writing is on the wall for centralised finance, and the world will be forced onto Bitcoin because it is the only neutral money that simply keeps working.

item.title: Bald in Bitcoin ΓÇô Bald in Bitcoin - Folge 13: Die stille Steuer ΓÇô Warum der Staat Inflation nicht bek├ñmpfen will
item.description:
Deine Eltern haben dir gesagt: Leg Geld zur├╝ck. Sparen lohnt sich.

Guter Rat ΓÇô damals. Aber heute ist etwas kaputt.

In Folge 12 haben wir gezeigt, warum dein Kontostand stabil aussehen kann und du trotzdem ├ñrmer wirst. Heute gehen wir einen Schritt weiter und stellen die unbequeme Frage dahinter: Warum wird Inflation nicht einfach abgestellt?

Die Antwort: Weil das System genau so gebaut ist.

DAS LERNST DU IN DIESER FOLGE

ΓÇô Warum Staaten gigantische Schulden durch Inflation heimlich entwerten ΓÇô Was finanzielle Repression bedeutet ΓÇô und warum das kein Verschw├╢rungsmythos ist ΓÇô Wer wirklich die Rechnung zahlt: die stille Steuer auf deine Ersparnisse ΓÇô Warum das 2%-Ziel der EZB keine zuf├ñllige Zahl ist ΓÇô Was Bitcoin als mengenm├ñ├ƒig begrenztes System damit zu tun hat

WEITERF├£HRENDE EMPFEHLUNGEN

Der Bitcoin-Standard ΓÇô Saifedean Ammous Schulden: Die ersten 5000 Jahre ΓÇô David Graeber Reinhart & Sbrancia: The Liquidation of Government Debt (Studie, englisch)

VOR-ORT-KURS ΓÇô 30. MAI 2026

Bitcoin f├╝r Einsteiger ΓÇô digitales Geld f├╝r analog Geborene. Kein Trading. Kein Hype. Einfach ein ruhiger, verst├ñndlicher Einstieg. https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin

Hinweis: In dieser Folge wird neben meiner Stimme eine KI-generierte Expertenstimme f├╝r technische Erkl├ñrpassagen eingesetzt. Alle Inhalte werden von mir recherchiert und gepr├╝ft.

ΓÜá∩╕Å Keine Finanzberatung. Ausschlie├ƒlich Bildung und pers├╢nliche Perspektive.

]]>

item.title: Epicenter - Learn about Crypto, Blockchain, Ethereum, Bitcoin and Distributed Technologies ΓÇô Why Only 3 Builders Control All of Ethereum
item.description:
In this episode, host Friederike Ernst is joined by Kubi Mensah, CEO and co-founder of Gattaca, the company behind Titan Builder. Kubi sheds light on the highly competitive and often opaque world of Ethereum block building, explaining how Gattaca evolved from a centralized exchange proprietary trading firm to one of the three dominant builders responsible for constructing the vast majority of Ethereum blocks today .
┬á



┬á
They dissect the true "journey of a transaction," revealing why over half of all Ethereum transaction value bypasses the public mempool in favor of private order flow auctions and MEV-protection services . Kubi explains the intricate mechanics of top-of-block bidding by high-frequency DeFi arbitrageurs, the necessity of extreme latency optimization, and the "flywheel effect" that makes block building a natural oligopoly . Finally, the discussion turns to the future of the Ethereum roadmap, unpacking how upcoming upgrades like ePBS (enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation) and FOCIL (Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion Lists) aim to permanently alter the power dynamics between block builders, validators, and originators .
┬á
┬á
Topics


  • 04:15 Transitioning from TradFi Trading to Ethereum Block Building
  • 09:30 Redefining the Builder: Relays and Order Flow Auctions
  • 15:00 Unpacking the Mempool: Public vs. Private Transactions
  • 21:45 The Anatomy of a Block & "Top-of-Block" Arbitrage
  • 27:10 How Builders Pay Proposers to Win Auctions
  • 35:20 The Oligopoly: Why Only 3 Builders Dominate Ethereum
  • 42:15 Trust in the Dark Forest: Handling Searcher Bundles
  • 49:00 The Convergence of Builders and Solvers
  • 55:30 The Impact of ePBS, FOCIL, and Pre-confirmations




Sponsors:



Lido V3 introduces stVaults: a modular staking infrastructure that lets builders and institutions deploy custom staking vaults, while staying anchored to stETH as a shared liquidity layer.Get started building with Lido V3 today: https://lido.fi/stvaults?mtm_campaign=epicenterBlock Space Forum: https://blockspace.forum/

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Weekly Update ΓÇô Bitcoin, Oil, Saylor and SpaceX: The Weekly Market Shock
item.description: <![CDATA[This week on The Weekly, David Sencil and Graham Stone break down a major set of stories moving Bitcoin and crypto markets.They discuss the reported Iran-US peace deal, the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, falling oil futures, and BitcoinΓÇÖs move above $66K. They also unpack Michael SaylorΓÇÖs controversial 32 BTC sale, his BTC Prague comments, MetaplanetΓÇÖs move into Bitcoin yield products, Bitcoin miners pivoting toward AI, AnthropicΓÇÖs Fable export-control controversy, and the SpaceX IPOΓÇÖs impact on crypto-based pre-IPO price discovery.A fast-moving episode covering macro, Bitcoin, AI, miners, IPOs, and the next stage of crypto market structure.]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Rails | Isabel Foxen Duke ΓÇô BIP 110 & The Future of Soft Forks | SUPER TESTNET
item.description:
One of Bitcoin's most prolific experimental developers, Super Testnet is known for building software at the frontier of Bitcoin research ΓÇö including notable contributions to BitVM, Drivechains, Lightning, and numerous prototypes that have pushed the boundaries of Bitcoin's design space.

More recently, he's become a prominent participant in discussions surrounding BIP 110, a proposed soft fork intended to restrict transaction patterns associated with Ordinal inscriptions.

While Super broadly agrees with the proposal's underlying goals, he argues its current restrictions are overly broad and could impact "legitimate" monetary uses of Bitcoin.

These concerns led him to share a User-Rejected Soft Fork proposal last week, creating a rare case study in how Bitcoin's consensus rules evolve in practice.

In more detail, Super Testnet & I discuss:

  • BIP 110, and why developers disagree over transaction filtering
  • Soft fork activation through a historical lens
  • Miner-Activated and User-Activated Soft Forks + the hybrid strategy proposed by BIP 110 advocates
  • Super's User-Rejected Soft Fork proposal and its game theoretical implications for BIP 110 activation
  • Novel signaling mechanisms for BIP 110 + their implications for the future of hashrate markets

This episode of Bitcoin Rails is brought to you by my sponsors:

LayerTwo Labs ΓÇö developing research, software, and technologies for scaling Bitcoin via the integration of Drivechains (BIP 300/301)

Hashi on Sui Network ΓÇö a primitive for executing Bitcoin Defi transactions, without having to trust a federated bridge or other centralized entity

BitBox ΓÇö an open-source Bitcoin-only hardware wallet, with smooth UX and no compromises on security. Check out Bitbox [dot] swiss and use code BITCOINRAILS to get a discount

]]>

Guerre mon├⌐taire, fin du privil├¿ge du dollar ou simple r├⌐├⌐quilibrage ? Pour ce deuxi├¿me et dernier volet, l'├⌐conomiste Jean-Marc Daniel sort des fronti├¿res fran├ºaises et d├⌐roule sa lecture du nouvel ├⌐chiquier mon├⌐taire mondial.


Guerre commerciale ou guerre mon├⌐taire ?

Privil├¿ge exorbitant du dollar, exc├⌐dents allemands et japonais, d├⌐mographie et bloc des ┬½ vieux ┬╗ contre bloc des ┬½ jeunes ┬╗, stablecoins et CLARITY Act, euro num├⌐rique, dollarisation des pays ├⌐mergents, Bitcoin hors syst├¿meΓÇª

Γ₧ñ Bitcoin a-t-il une valeur intrins├¿que ? Le grand d├⌐saccord avec Jean-Marc Daniel

item.title: Thank God for Bitcoin ΓÇô To The Unknown Pod Episode 42: Bitcoin and A World At War
item.description:
In this episode, Jordan Bush and Ryan Finlay explore the complex intersections of global conflict, economic warfare, and Bitcoin's role as a resilient alternative in a world at war. They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>

In dieser Episode sprechen wir ├╝ber das Thema Nummer eins, bei dem Anf├ñnger die gr├╢├ƒten (und teuersten) Fehler machen: Sicherheit. Max erkl├ñrt am K├╝chentisch, warum wir bei Kryptow├ñhrungen unsere eigene Bank sind und welche immense Verantwortung das mit sich bringt.

In dieser Folge erf├ñhrst du:┬áΓÇó Warum eine ΓÇ₧WalletΓÇ£ eigentlich gar keine Coins enth├ñlt, sondern nur Schl├╝ssel. ΓÇó Den Unterschied zwischen dem Public Key (deiner Kontonummer) und dem Private Key (deinem Tresorschl├╝ssel). ΓÇó Was eine Seed-Phrase ist und warum diese 12 oder 24 W├╢rter ├╝ber alles entscheiden. ΓÇó Die wichtigste, goldene Regel der Krypto-Welt, gegen die du niemals versto├ƒen darfst.

Diese Episode basiert auf Kapitel 2 des Einsteiger-Romans ΓÇ₧Krypto verstehen ΓÇô sicher starten. Band 1ΓÇ£ von Sascha Swen Heiter. Verantwortung steht ├╝ber Spekulation. Bleib sicher und investiere mit K├╢pfchen!

Besonders bei steuerlichen Themen ist zu beachten, dass die steuerliche Behandlung von Kryptow├ñhrungen sehr komplex ist.
